summary: Terminal artifact validation now rejects parseable payloads unless they contain a recognized terminal status or a recognized terminal disposition. A focused regression test covers an arbitrary parseable object while preserving established status-based and disposition-only result shapes.

gap_statuses.requirement: A terminal result must have a recognized terminal status/disposition
gap_statuses.evidence: Terminal loading now requires membership in the recognized terminal-status or terminal-disposition sets; empty and arbitrary dispositions cannot become authoritative evidence.

gap_statuses.requirement: Unrecognized parseable terminal evidence must be covered
gap_statuses.evidence: test_pr_resolver_rejects_unrecognized_terminal_evidence writes a valid JSON object without terminal semantics and asserts rejection plus the compact validation failure.

targeted_checks.notes: The first run exposed four established reenter-gate cases that use recognized terminal statuses while disposition derivation is context-dependent. Validation was narrowed to accept recognized statuses or a recognized disposition.
79 Python tests passed with five non-blocking pytest marker warnings. Automatic frontend phase passed 63 files and 972 tests with 238 skipped. Diff hygiene passed.
